planting_details.assumptions_or_notes: Rainfed smallholder system; planting timed to onset of long rains (mid-Mar–early Apr). H614 hybrid suited to Kitale highlands. Density approximates Kenya extension recommendations for high potential areas (≈53,000 plants/ha), implemented as 75 cm rows with ~25 cm within-row spacing (implied by PPOP=5.33). Planting depth set to 5 cm for moist soils. No specific row orientation data; set PLRD=-99. Year-specific rainfall not consulted; date chosen as representative median of typical window.

events.Assumptions_or_Notes: Basal DAP at planting, banded beneath the surface near the seed to reduce seed burn and improve P availability; typical smallholder practice in Kitale. Product: Diammonium phosphate (DAP), grade 18-46-0. Product rate assumed ~100 kg/ha, giving N = 100 × 0.18 = 18.0 kg N/ha and P = 100 × 0.46 × 0.4364 ≈ 20.1 kg P/ha (K = 0). Placement depth ~5 cm, slightly to the side of the seed. This event reflects common local use of DAP as the basal fertilizer.

Topdress CAN at knee-high stage, broadcast by hand without incorporation; standard smallholder practice for N in the Kenyan highlands. Product: Calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN), grade ~26-0-0. Product rate set to ~142.3 kg/ha to deliver exactly 37.0 kg N/ha (142.3 × 0.26 ≈ 37.0). No P or K supplied. Timing at 35 DAP matches typical early topdress window during the long rains.
